Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: per-channel FX now correctly isolates target channel only

Root cause: the worklet had two independent mute mask paths that
overwrote each other. setMuteMask (user mute/solo) and updateMainMask
(isolation exclusion) both called applyMuteMask_ on the main module,
so whichever arrived last won. When the mixer sent setMuteMask with
all-channels-active, it undid the isolation exclusion — causing ALL
channels to render through the effect chain.

Fix: the worklet now tracks both masks separately:
- userMuteMask: user mute/solo state (bit=1 means active)
- isolatedBits: channels routed to isolation slots (bit=1 means isolated)

Effective main mask = userMuteMask & ~isolatedBits, so a channel
is audible in the main output ONLY if the user hasn't muted it AND
it's not being rendered by an isolation slot.

Isolation slots also respect user mutes: slot effective mask =
channelMask & userMuteMask.

Additional safety:
- applyMuteMask_ now returns success/failure boolean
- addIsolationSlot_ aborts if muteFunc extraction fails (prevents
  unmuted isolation module from rendering all channels)
- addIsolationSlot_ aborts if applyMuteMask_ fails at runtime
- isolationError message sent to engine on failure, which cleans
  up the slot mask so main module doesn't incorrectly mute channels
- Both masks reset on play() for clean state per song

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

metadata (src/mcp_metadata.rs, mcp-metadata.json), and all firewall tests.

- Remove mcp-firewall CLI subcommand
- Simplify create wizard to MCP-level selection (no tool-level metadata)
- Update 1ES compiler to use is_custom_mcp() check
- Remove terminal_size dependency (only used by deleted MCP tool selector)

All MCPs are now handled through the MCP Gateway (MCPG) instead of
the custom firewall proxy.

* feat: add MCP Gateway (MCPG) support with SafeOutputs HTTP server

Add infrastructure for the gh-aw-mcpg gateway integration:

- Add MCPG_VERSION and MCPG_IMAGE constants in common.rs
- Add {{ mcpg_version }} and {{ mcpg_image }} template markers
- Replace generate_firewall_config() with generate_mcpg_config() that
  produces MCPG-compatible JSON (mcpServers + gateway sections)
- SafeOutputs always included as HTTP backend via host.docker.internal
- Custom MCPs (with command:) become stdio servers in MCPG config
- Add host.docker.internal to CORE_ALLOWED_HOSTS for AWF container
  to reach host-side MCPG
- Add mcp-http subcommand: serves SafeOutputs over HTTP using rmcp's
  StreamableHttpService with axum, API key auth, and health endpoint

Replace the legacy MCP firewall pipeline steps with MCPG architecture:

- Replace 'Prepare MCP firewall config' with 'Prepare MCPG config'

- Add 'Stop MCPG and SafeOutputs' cleanup step (condition: always)

- Log MCPG config template before API key substitution to avoid leaking
  MCP_GATEWAY_API_KEY to pipeline logs (ADO secret masking only applies
  in subsequent steps, not the current step's output)

  for standalone pipelines (not conditionally)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden SafeOutputs HTTP server security

- Use subtle::ConstantTimeEq for API key comparison to prevent
  timing side-channel attacks from a compromised AWF container
- Fail loudly (panic) when /dev/urandom is unavailable instead of
  silently generating a weak time-based key
- Add doc comment on SafeOutputs Clone confirming concurrent safety:
  only contains immutable PathBuf fields, file I/O opens fresh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: improve MCPG reliability and diagnostics

- Pre-cleanup stale mcpg container before docker run to prevent
  retry failures when a previous run was interrupted (OOM/SIGKILL
  leaves container behind despite --rm)

  migration path from removed built-in MCPs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: improve SafeOutputs HTTP server robustness

- Replace panic! with anyhow bail via .context() for /dev/urandom
  failure — panics in async contexts crash the Tokio task instead of
  propagating cleanly through the Result chain
- Bind to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0 — MCPG runs with --network
  host so localhost is sufficient, reducing exposure on shared agents

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  to localhost — MCPG runs with --network host on Linux where
  host.docker.internal is not auto-injected in host network mode
- Gateway domain remains host.docker.internal (used by AWF container
  which runs in bridge network mode with --enable-host-access)
- Add docker rm -f pre-cleanup before MCPG container start to handle

  boolean-enabled formats

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add MCPG client auth and generate API key early

MCPG enforces authentication on incoming client requests. The agent's
mcp-config.json now includes an Authorization header with the gateway
API key. To support this, the MCP_GATEWAY_API_KEY is generated in the
'Prepare MCPG config' step (as an ADO secret variable) instead of the
'Start MCP Gateway' step, making it available when writing both the
MCPG server config and the agent's client config.

Also updates MCPG startup step to reference the ADO variable via
$(MCP_GATEWAY_API_KEY) instead of the shell-local variable.

[claude] feat: v2.0.0 dual-proxy gateway rewrite

BREAKING CHANGE: Complete architectural rewrite. HermesClaw no longer
calls AI APIs directly — it acts as a pure iLink proxy that routes
messages to Hermes and OpenClaw gateways.

Architecture:
- HermesClaw is the sole iLink poller (eliminates token conflicts)
- Two local HTTP proxies (:19998 for Hermes, :19999 for OpenClaw)
- Each gateway polls its own proxy, believing it is the real iLink API
- sendmessage replies forwarded to real iLink with token replacement

Changes:
- hermesclaw.py: rewritten (~580 lines, down from 763)
  - Removed all media handling (AES/CDN/pycryptodome)
  - Removed direct Hermes API calls (/v1/chat/completions)
  - Added MessageQueue with Event-based long-polling
  - Added make_proxy_handler() factory for dual proxy servers
  - Added BrokenPipeError handling for gateway timeouts
- install.sh: rewritten with dual-gateway detection matrix
  - Detects OpenClaw clawbot + Hermes weixin gateway
  - Patches baseUrl/WEIXIN_BASE_URL to proxy ports
  - 8-case detection (both/either/neither installed)
- README.md: updated pain point, architecture diagram, prerequisites
- requirements.txt: requests + python-dotenv (removed pycryptodome)
- tests/: 58 tests (core routing, proxy behavior, recovery/backoff)

commit.message:
feat(sensor): Pi-owned firmware status + persistent RestoreSensor

Two improvements to the Firmware Update Status sensor:

1. Pi is authoritative: firmware_update.py now reads the 'status' field
   directly from the Pi's GET /api/update response instead of re-deriving
   state from version strings.  Falls back to boolean + last_checked_utc
   logic for old Pi firmware that predates the 'status' field.

2. Persistent: CanteraFirmwareUpdateStatusSensor now extends RestoreSensor
   and restores its last known value on HA restart, so the sensor never
   reverts to 'not_checked' while the Pi is temporarily offline during
   an HA reload.  Invalid restored values are silently ignored.

tests in test_sensor.py.  288/288 passing.

* Fix get_db: yield+close connections, drop per-request WAL pragma

The per-request PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL was a write operation that
could conflict with concurrent reads under 5 parallel requests.
WAL is already set once at init_db() and persists per-database.

Changed get_db to a generator that yields the connection and closes
it in the finally block, preventing connection leaks. Added
PRAGMA query_only=ON for safety.

Added 5 regression tests for split endpoints (street-stats,
ev-summary, top-hands, ev-node-data with pool+all filters).

* Fix SQLite cross-thread close: add check_same_thread=False

FastAPI runs dependency cleanup (conn.close()) in a different thread
than the one that created and used the connection. SQLite blocks this
by default. Safe here because each request gets its own connection
and WAL mode handles concurrent reads.

commit.message:
Fix SID format pattern scroll jitter: reduce ScriptProcessor buffer 2048→512

Root cause: websid's ScriptProcessorNode ran with 2048-sample buffer (~46ms).
The C64 emulator only advances inside onaudioprocess, so position could only
update every ~46ms. GT Ultra's AudioWorklet uses 128-sample blocks (~2.9ms),
giving 16x finer granularity — explaining why GT scroll is rock-solid.

Fix: set adapter.setProcessorBufSize(512) before ScriptNodePlayer.initialize().
512 samples @ 44.1kHz = ~11.6ms between updates — 4x improvement over default.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
